Content_Sketch: You don't arrest people. You collect them.
Illustration_Note: Stickman cop silhouette in dark room, badge on belt, phone glow in jacket pocket, weight line overlay

Content_Sketch: Fourteen on your phone right now. Six exist on paper. The rest don't exist at all.
Illustration_Note: Cop in dark jacket checking phone, split visual: 6 file folders vs 8 empty silhouettes

Content_Sketch: Same charge. Different outcome. Your choice.
Illustration_Note: POV from behind desk looking at person in chair, charge sheet on table, two outcomes implied

Content_Sketch: Academy. CI program briefing. Instructor calls it asset management. You write it down.
Illustration_Note: Training classroom, instructor at whiteboard with 'CI Program' written, recruits in rows, cop taking notes

Content_Sketch: First patrol. First arrest. You book them. Sergeant shrugs. One arrest.
Illustration_Note: Cop at booking desk processing arrest normally, sergeant behind desk unimpressed, whiteboard with low numbers

Content_Sketch: Senior detective pulls you aside. Shows the second phone. This is how you get numbers.
Illustration_Note: Senior detective in wrinkled jacket showing prepaid phone to cop in hallway, away from others, conspiratorial lean

Content_Sketch: First time watching a flip. Booking room. Kid crying. Senior says three words. Kid nods. Work this off. First time hearing it. Sounds like a deal.
Illustration_Note: Booking room: senior detective standing over crying kid in chair, charge sheet on table, kid nodding, cop watching from doorway

Content_Sketch: Your turn. Booking room. This one won't look at you. Hands shaking. Keeps asking if this clears it.
Illustration_Note: Cop standing over CI anchor in booking room chair, CI in worn hoodie hands shaking, avoiding eye contact, charge sheet between them

Content_Sketch: Work this off or do the time. You explain carefully. You mean it when you say one buy.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's mouth explaining deal, CI anchor's shaking hands gripping chair edge, genuine exchange

Content_Sketch: First deploy. No wire. No training. They buy. You wait in the parking lot. Engine running.
Illustration_Note: Split: CI anchor walking toward target house alone / cop sitting in car in dark parking lot, phone in hand, engine on

Content_Sketch: They come back. Shaking. You file the bust. Their name is not in it. Commendation. Sergeant nods.
Illustration_Note: CI anchor returning to parking lot shaking / cop at precinct desk filing report, sergeant nodding, commendation letter

Content_Sketch: You own them. First time. Clinical. Professional distance. Check the phone. Not the department one. You look around first.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop checking prepaid phone, glancing around before looking at screen, badge visible, posture guarded

Content_Sketch: Second flip. Faster. Smaller charge. Bag of weed. Leverage is leverage. Third. Fourth. List grows.
Illustration_Note: Montage: different civilians in same booking room chair, cop's posture increasingly dominant, folders stacking up

Content_Sketch: First week you remember their names. Third week you remember their charges. You don't remember when that changed.
Illustration_Note: Cop at desk with phone, scrolling contacts - names blur into charge codes, time distortion visual

Content_Sketch: Department knows about six. Others live on the second phone only. You check it before the department one now. Stopped looking around.
Illustration_Note: Split: 6 neat files on desk / cop checking prepaid phone openly, no more glancing around, casual corruption

Content_Sketch: CI anchor calls at 2AM. Not to report. To ask if they're safe. Brings names you didn't ask for. Trying to stay useful. Trying to stay alive.
Illustration_Note: Cop in bed, phone glowing 2AM, CI anchor's voice implied, cop's expression flat, notepad on nightstand

Content_Sketch: They hesitate. Longer this time. I think I'm done. You slide the charge sheet back across the table.
Illustration_Note: Booking room: CI anchor pushing back, cop calmly sliding charge sheet forward, power dynamic visible, silence

Content_Sketch: When does this end? After this one you're clear. You already know that's not true.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's face saying 'you're clear' - dot eyes cold, CI anchor's shaking hands accepting, false promise

Content_Sketch: Work this off. No explanation. Three words. Next. You stop seeing faces. You see output.
Illustration_Note: Rapid cuts: different CIs in same chair, same three words, cop's posture unchanging, faces blur to silhouettes

Content_Sketch: CI anchor calls at 2AM the night before. You don't answer. New target. You know the target carries. You do not tell them.
Illustration_Note: Phone ringing in dark room, cop looking at screen - not answering, then briefing scene with target file showing weapon notation

Content_Sketch: Phone rings. Not the department phone. The other one. You wait for breathing. There is none.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop holding phone to ear, prepaid phone, silence visual - empty waveform, cop's face going flat

Content_Sketch: Line stays open. You don't hang up. You wait anyway.
Illustration_Note: Close on phone screen - call timer running, cop holding phone, not moving, waiting for sound that won't come

Content_Sketch: Delete the thread. Remove the number. Close the file. They were never yours. You still have thirteen.
Illustration_Note: Cop methodically deleting messages, removing contact, closing folder, phone showing 13 contacts, cold inventory

Content_Sketch: You own them. Not metaphor anymore.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's face, phone in hand showing 13 names, dot eyes possessive, signature line weight

Content_Sketch: New detective watches you flip someone. Don't ask. Tell. They flip their first CI alone. Your words. Your phrasing.
Illustration_Note: New detective in booking room mimicking cop's posture and gestures, same chair, same charge sheet, mirror of Phase 2

Content_Sketch: They ask if this is how it's supposed to work. You hand them the phone. Next week they don't ask anymore.
Illustration_Note: Cop handing prepaid phone to new detective, symbolic transfer, new detective holding it like cop held it in Phase 2

Content_Sketch: Thirteen numbers. Remember charges. Not faces. You own them. Check pocket for phone. Phone gone. Hand still reaches.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's hand reaching into empty jacket pocket, phantom phone, dot eyes empty, identity complete

Content_Sketch: Interrogation room. Someone new sits down. Same chair. Same charge. Same offer. You already know what they'll say.
Illustration_Note: Same booking room as Phase 2, new person in chair mirroring CI anchor's posture, cop standing, folder in hand

Content_Sketch: You own them. You don't remember when that stopped being a figure of speech.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's face, dot eyes empty, final weight - same room same posture same emptiness, loop closed
